what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-spec for view A8, describing a planned desktop/mobile surface titled "Semantic zoom \u2014 far vs near", listing the UI addresses it targets (canvas zoomhint and toolbar fit), the components it builds to, and build-time constraints (a design lint gate and a single token source).
observations: It is a surface-spec JSON with id A8, area A, and status planned.
Its home_journey is "_unrouted" and both journeys and cross_journeys are empty arrays.
It declares two addresses: ui://canvas/zoomhint and ui://toolbar/fit.
It builds to components canvas and toolbar.
The build_to block includes a design_lint command and a tokens source rule.
The file notes it was built by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: journeys and cross_journeys are empty even though the file describes them as the address-refs that carry the web.
Two concrete ui:// addresses anchor the surface to canvas and toolbar.
Build constraints mix a lint gate (FORM gate) with a single CSS token source rule.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Breaks its own described convention by leaving journeys/cross_journeys empty while supplying addresses in a separate array.
concerns: Empty journeys and cross_journeys conflict with the file's explanation that they carry the web of address-refs.
Status is planned with no routed journeys, so integration path is undefined.
The note "never invent an address" implies external validation is required, but the file contains no validation evidence.
notes: A planned desktop/mobile surface spec for semantic zoom that anchors two UI addresses (canvas zoomhint and toolbar fit) but has not yet been routed into any journey.
questions: Why are journeys and cross_journeys empty if they are meant to carry the address-ref web?
What specific behavior does "Semantic zoom \u2014 far vs near" describe?
What is the FORM gate and which rules does --fail-on enforce?
How are ui://canvas/zoomhint and ui://toolbar/fit resolved or validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a surface spec with a journey spine and address-refs carrying the web; the actual content supplies the surface spec and addresses but omits the journey web, so it only partially matches.
apparent_themes: Separation of surface definition from journey routing.
Design-system consistency via a single token source and a lint gate.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a reusable, buildable surface for semantic zoom by anchoring it to specific UI addresses and components, while deferring the journey routing to a later step.
novelty: The explicit "never invent an address" builder note and the split between a populated addresses array and empty journeys/cross_journeys arrays.
contribution: A structured surface definition that records which UI addresses and components a semantic-zoom feature should bind to, plus build constraints (lint target and token source) that a fused system could keep as a build contract.
summary_for_embedding: A8.json is a planned surface specification for a desktop/mobile semantic-zoom view that lists addresses ui://canvas/zoomhint and ui://toolbar/fit, targets canvas and toolbar components, and declares design-lint and token-source build constraints.
intention_for_embedding: It was generated to capture the build contract and UI address anchors for a semantic zoom surface before journey routing is assigned.
